question_title: Will at least one 2025–26 UEFA Champions League quarter-final first-leg match finish with four or more total goals before May 1, 2026?
summary: The question asks whether at least one of four 2025-26 UCL QF first-leg matches (April 7-8, 2026) will finish with 4+ total goals. The matchups are: Sporting vs Arsenal, Real Madrid vs Bayern Munich, Barcelona vs Atletico Madrid, and PSG vs Liverpool. Historical analysis of 24 QF first-leg matches across 6 seasons shows a per-match rate of 37.5% for 4+ goals, and 4 of 6 seasons had at least one such match (66.7%). Poisson modeling with matchup-specific expected goals (λ=2.2-3.3) gives P(at least one) = 76.5%, while the simple empirical rate gives 84.7%. This season's CL has been exceptionally high-scoring (R16 averaged 4.12 goals/match), and Real Madrid vs Bayern Munich is historically a high-scoring fixture. Even conservative Poisson estimates (λ=1.8-3.0) yield ~67%. The "at least one of four" structure strongly favors YES.

factors.description: Historical base rate: 4 of 6 recent CL seasons with two-legged QFs had at least one first-leg match with 4+ goals (66.7%)
Per-match empirical rate of 37.5% (9/24 matches) for 4+ goals in QF first legs, yielding P(at least 1 of 4)=84.7% via independence
Real Madrid vs Bayern Munich is a historically high-scoring fixture; both teams scored heavily in R16 (RM: 5 goals, Bayern: 10 goals aggregate)
This season's CL has been exceptionally high-scoring: R16 averaged 4.12 goals per match, with Barcelona scoring 7 and Bayern 6 in single legs
QF first legs tend to be more cautious than R16: teams at this stage play more conservatively, reducing scoring expectations vs R16 rates
Poisson model with matchup-specific expected goals (λ=2.2-3.3) gives P(at least one 4+ match) = 76.5%; conservative λs (1.8-3.0) give 66.9%

reasoning:
**Forecast: 0.82 (82%)**

Key reasoning:
- **Four QF first-leg matches** (April 7-8): Sporting vs Arsenal, Real Madrid vs Bayern Munich, Barcelona vs Atletico Madrid, PSG vs Liverpool
- **Historical base rate**: 4/6 recent seasons had at least one 4+ goal QF first leg (66.7%)
- **Per-match probability**: 9/24 historical QF first legs had 4+ goals (37.5%), giving P(≥1 of 4) ≈ 85%
- **Poisson modeling**: Best-estimate matchup-specific lambdas yield 76.5%; conservative estimates give 67%
- **This year's matchups are strongly attacking**: Real Madrid vs Bayern Munich alone has ~42% chance of 4+ goals. Combined with Barcelona vs Atletico and PSG vs Liverpool, the aggregate probability is high
- **High-scoring season**: The R16 averaged 4.12 goals/match, well above historical norms

The "at least one of four" structure is the decisive factor — even modest per-match probabilities compound to a high aggregate probability of YES.
sources_consulted: [European Cup and UEFA Champions League records and statistics - Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/European_Cup_and_UEFA_Champions_League_records_and_statistics)
resolution_criteria: This question resolves to yes if at least one official 2025–26 UEFA Champions League quarter-final first-leg match played in April 2026 finishes with a total of four or more goals after regulation time. It resolves to no otherwise. The primary source is UEFA’s fixtures and results page at https://www.uefa.com/uefachampionsleague/fixtures-results/
fine_print: Count goals scored in the match result after 90 minutes plus stoppage time. Do not count penalty shootout goals. Candidate Sources: UEFA knockout-phase dates page https://www.uefa.com/uefachampionsleague/news/02a2-1ffe3d954170-60c0b7443c85-1000--uefa-champions-league-round-of-16-quarter-final-and-semi-/
